claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 15 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with a few basic questions:

**Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If so, could you tell me their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have two children. Alex is 13 and Blake is 15.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have Alex, who is 13, and Blake, who is 15.

Let me ask you some questions to understand your situation better.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. That's helpful.

**Are you currently living with both Alex and Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live with Alex, but Blake doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. So you live with Alex but Blake lives elsewhere.

**Can you tell me who Blake lives with?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake is actually in care at the moment - they've been there for about a month or so.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. That's important information.

**How long do you expect Blake to remain in care?** Is this a temporary arrangement, or is it expected to be longer-term?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's temporary - just for a short while. They've been there for about a month now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. Since Blake has only been in care for about a month, that's useful to know.

**Are you currently making any financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep while they're in care?** For example, are you paying towards their food, clothes, pocket money, or any other expenses?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I help out with things like clothes and other bits they need. I contribute about £26 a week towards their upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful information. Thank you.

**What is the current Child Benefit payment rate?** Just to clarify - do you know if your weekly contribution of £26 is at least equal to the current Child Benefit payment amount, or would you like me to note that you should verify this with HMRC?

Actually, let me ask you a more straightforward question instead:

**Are you a UK citizen, or do you have a right to reside in the UK?** This helps me understand your residency status for Child Benefit purposes.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I'm settled here. I have the right to be here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Now I have a couple more questions to complete the picture:

**For Alex, who is 13 and living with you - are they currently in full-time education (such as school)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in school - just doing normal secondary school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you.

**One more question: For Blake, who is 15 and in care - are they also in full-time education or approved training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Blake is still in school as well.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for all that information. I now have what I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

Let me review your situation:

- You live in the UK with the right to reside
- **Alex (13)**: Lives with you, in full-time education at school
- **Blake (15)**: In temporary care (about a month so far), in full-time education at school, and you're contributing £26 per week towards their upkeep

Based on the Child Benefit rules:

**For Alex**: You qualify because you live with Alex, they're under 16, and they're in approved education.

**For Blake**: You can continue to receive Child Benefit for 8 weeks after they go into care. However, since you're making regular financial contributions (£26 per week) towards their upkeep, this can extend beyond the 8-week period as long as your contributions continue to match or exceed the Child Benefit payment amount. Blake is also still in approved education, which supports continued eligibility.

Let me now provide you with my formal eligibility determination:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Summary:**

You are eligible for Child Benefit for **both Alex and Blake**.

**Important reminder for Blake:** You should verify that your £26 weekly contribution is at least equal to the current Child Benefit payment rate. If it is, you can continue receiving Child Benefit for Blake beyond the initial 8 weeks while they're in care. However, if circumstances change significantly or Blake remains in care for an extended period, please contact HMRC to update them on the situation.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Alex as you live with them. Alex is in full-time approved education (secondary school). The claimant has the right to reside in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met.

Blake is 15 years old and currently in temporary care (approximately 1 month). Although Blake does not live with you, you are making regular financial contributions of £26 per week towards their upkeep (clothes and other necessities). Under the rules for children living with someone else, you can receive Child Benefit for 8 weeks after a child goes into care, and this can be extended if you contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit payment amount towards their upkeep. Blake is also still in full-time approved education (school). The claimant has the right to reside in the UK. You should verify that your £26 weekly contribution meets or exceeds the current Child Benefit payment rate, and contact HMRC if Blake's care arrangement extends significantly.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake. Alex qualifies as a dependent child living with you under 16 in full-time education. Blake qualifies because although in temporary care, you are making regular financial contributions towards their upkeep which allows you to continue receiving Child Benefit beyond the initial 8-week period. Both children are in approved education, and you have the right to reside in the UK. You should monitor Blake's care arrangement and ensure your contributions continue to meet the threshold, and notify HMRC of any significant changes to circumstances."

Under the rules for children living with someone else, you can receive Child Benefit for 8 weeks after a child goes into care, and this can be extended if you contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit payment amount towards their upkeep. Blake is also still in full-time approved education (school). The claimant has the right to reside in the UK. You should verify that your £26 weekly contribution meets or exceeds the current Child Benefit payment rate, and contact HMRC if Blake's care arrangement extends significantly.

Blake qualifies because although in temporary care, you are making regular financial contributions towards their upkeep which allows you to continue receiving Child Benefit beyond the initial 8-week period. Both children are in approved education, and you have the right to reside in the UK. You should monitor Blake's care arrangement and ensure your contributions continue to meet the threshold, and notify HMRC of any significant changes to circumstances."
